skill.name: senior-data-scientist
skill.description: Data science guidance for statistical modeling, experiment design, causal inference, feature engineering, model evaluation, production ML systems, and stakeholder communication.
skill.summary: Guide advanced analytics, experiments, feature engineering, and production model evaluation.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-crypto and reconnaissance matches, but file review found generic data science text and placeholder scripts rather than cryptographic or network-scanning behavior. The real risk is moderate: SKILL.md contains shell and deployment command examples, and the package includes executable Python scripts, so users should review commands before running them.

medium_findings.title: Shell and deployment command examples require user review
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md includes bash examples for running local scripts and common development, Docker, Kubernetes, and Helm commands. They are documentation examples, not hidden execution logic, but they can mutate local projects or infrastructure if copied without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are directly visible and include deployment commands. Risk is moderate because the skill documents commands but does not execute them automatically.

medium_findings.title: Executable placeholder scripts accept filesystem paths
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The package includes Python command-line scripts with input, output, config, and verbose arguments. Current implementations only build a config object and print status JSON, but future use or modification could affect files selected by user-supplied paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts and argparse path parameters are present, but the reviewed code does not read, write, delete, execute subprocesses, or access the network.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm matches are false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations contain descriptions such as design, production practices, data science scope, and script names. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, custom crypto, or password hashing logic in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary prose and CLI descriptions at the flagged lines, not weak cryptographic primitives or crypto API calls.

low_findings.title: Network reconnaissance matches are false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged reference lines discuss resource awareness in performance design. No evidence found of port scanning, host discovery, socket probing, or network reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain generic performance wording and no network utilities, endpoints, sockets, or scanning behavior.

low_findings.title: Several external command matches are documentation references
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Some static external-command hits point to inline file references and resource lists, not shell execution. No evidence found that these lines execute Ruby backticks, subprocess calls, or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown inline code and resource names at these lines. They are not executable blocks or code paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented infrastructure commands
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md documents Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, and log commands. These are legitimate examples but should not be run against production without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present in a bash block. They are not automatically executed by the skill.

content.value_statement: Teams need rigorous analytics plans that connect statistics, models, and business decisions. This skill provides structured guidance for experiments, features, evaluation, and production readiness.
actual_capabilities: Frames statistical modeling, causal inference, A/B testing, and time series analysis workflows.
Provides reference guidance for experiment design frameworks and production analytics practices.
Outlines feature engineering patterns, monitoring, deployment, and observability considerations.
Includes placeholder Python command-line scripts for experiment design, feature engineering, and model evaluation.
Supports stakeholder communication around trade-offs, reliability, performance, and business impact.
limitations: The bundled scripts are placeholders and do not implement real data processing logic.
It does not validate datasets, model quality, or statistical assumptions automatically.
It requires users to supply project context, data definitions, and success metrics.
Deployment commands are examples and must be reviewed before use in production systems.

use_cases.title: Design an Experiment Plan
use_cases.description: Create hypotheses, metrics, sample size considerations, and decision rules for an A/B test.

use_cases.title: Plan Feature Engineering
use_cases.description: Identify feature sources, transformations, leakage risks, validation checks, and monitoring needs.

use_cases.title: Review Model Readiness
use_cases.description: Assess evaluation metrics, drift monitoring, retraining plans, operational risks, and stakeholder reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Analysis Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured data science workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a data science analysis plan for this business question: [question]. Include data needs, assumptions, methods, and deliverables.

prompt_templates.title: Design an A/B Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an A/B test for [product change]. Define hypotheses, primary metrics, guardrail metrics, segmentation, risks, and launch criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a Predictive Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this model plan for [use case]. Cover metrics, validation strategy, bias checks, drift monitoring, and production readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Causal Inference Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analytics design with confounding risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this causal inference question: [question]. Recommend identification strategy, assumptions, diagnostics, sensitivity checks, and stakeholder explanation.

output_examples.input: Design an experiment for a new onboarding flow.
output: Hypothesis, target population, primary metric, guardrail metrics, and segmentation plan.
Sample size considerations, exposure rules, ramp plan, and stopping criteria.
Risk register covering novelty effects, instrumentation gaps, and metric interference.

output_examples.input: Review a churn prediction model before launch.
output: Evaluation checklist with precision, recall, calibration, stability, and subgroup performance.
Production readiness notes for feature freshness, monitoring, retraining, and alert ownership.
Stakeholder summary translating model trade-offs into operational actions.

output_examples.input: Plan features for a demand forecasting project.
output: Candidate feature groups for seasonality, promotions, inventory, geography, and external drivers.
Leakage checks, backtesting design, missing data handling, and monitoring requirements.

best_practices: Provide clear business goals, metric definitions, data windows, and constraints before requesting analysis.
Ask for assumptions, validation checks, and failure modes in every statistical or modeling plan.
Review any suggested command, deployment step, or script execution before running it.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the placeholder scripts as complete production pipelines.
Do not request model conclusions without sharing data provenance, sampling rules, and metric definitions.
Do not run example deployment commands against live infrastructure without approval.

faq.question: Does this skill run statistical analysis automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides analysis design and review. Users must provide data, code, and validation results.

faq.question: Can it help with A/B testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure hypotheses, metrics, sample planning, guardrails, and interpretation criteria.

faq.question: Does it include working production scripts?
faq.answer: No. The included Python scripts are placeholders that parse arguments and return simple status output.

faq.question: Can it support causal inference work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help frame assumptions, identification strategies, diagnostics, and sensitivity checks.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private datasets?
faq.answer: The files show no network exfiltration logic, but users should avoid sharing sensitive raw data unnecessarily.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
